question.label: Settling in
question.name: What’s been most on your mind at work lately, Sam?
question.description: Safe opener that lets Sam set context before anything specific is raised.

answer: I can do the tasks, but I still second-guess whether my approach matches team expectations.
note: He can do the tasks but is second-guessing whether his approach matches team expectations, which signals mild uncertainty and low steadiness.

question.label: Team expectations
question.name: When you say 'team expectations,' what part feels hardest to read?
question.description: Drills into the specific expectation gap he named so the manager can remove ambiguity.

answer: Local setup is better now, but I still lose time when the design and API details do not match.
note: He names a specific mismatch between design and API details that is costing time, which signals mild clarity friction.

question.label: Design API mismatch
question.name: When design and API details do not match, what is usually unclear first?
question.description: Drills into the mismatch he named so the manager can identify the exact ambiguity to remove.

answer: I am not always sure who should approve what before I push.
note: He names approval ownership before pushing as the specific ambiguity, which is a mild negative clarity signal; [THREAD-DEFERRED] 'who should approve what before I push' not drilled because drill cap requires advancing the arc.

question.label: Done definition
question.name: What does “done” mean to you on your current tickets?
question.description: Checks whether his working definition of completion matches the team’s expectation.

answer: I usually wait too long before asking because I want to solve things myself first.
note: He describes delaying questions because he is unsure when to ask, which signals a strong lack of clarity about expectations and escalation.

question.label: Wait too long
question.name: When you wait too long to ask, what are you usually waiting to know first?
question.description: Drills into his 'wait too long' pattern to surface the specific uncertainty he is trying to resolve alone.

answer: When feedback is specific and fast, I move much quicker.
note: He names a specific missing condition—fast, specific feedback—that currently limits his speed, which is a strong clarity gap; the fact that he moves quickly once he has it shows underlying engagement.

question.label: Thread follow
question.name: feedback specific fast, — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
question.description: Runtime thread-follow injected because planner did not follow substantive answer.

answer: I still feel fuzzy on where quality bars differ between quick fixes and core flows.
note: He names a specific clarity gap in quality expectations between quick fixes and core flows, with mild drag on engagement from ongoing fuzziness; [THREAD-DEFERRED] quality bars differ between quick fixes and core flows.

question.label: Handoff gaps
question.name: Where do the handoffs still feel fuzzy?
question.description: Maps the exact points in the onboarding flow where ownership or next steps are unclear.

answer: Pairing once or twice a week would probably prevent me from getting stuck silently.
note: Names a missing support mechanism — regular pairing to avoid getting stuck silently — which is a strong clarity and growth deficit signal; [THREAD-DEFERRED-WINDDOWN] pairing once or twice a week as a support need.

question.label: Support cadence
question.name: What kind of check-in from me would help you most over the next two weeks?
question.description: Clarifies the support rhythm that would help him move faster without waiting too long for reassurance.

answer: If we define what good looks like for my next two tickets, I will feel much steadier.
note: Names a specific missing support — clear definitions of what good looks like on the next two tickets — which signals an ongoing clarity gap and lack of growth-enabling guidance.

question.label: Fastest unblock
question.name: What would make the next two weeks feel steadier for you?
question.description: Ends by narrowing to the most useful support or change to carry forward from the conversation.

answer: I want to start by pairing on my next ticket so I can see the approval bar in practice.
note: He names a concrete support move — pairing on the next ticket to see the approval bar in practice — which signals a constructive path to steadier work and learning.
